How does this change storm surge?

So basically, the way the hurricane is turning and where it hits determines which area receives more storm surge in relation to it... When it's blowing in a direction out to sea, it takes water with it, then dumps it on the areas where it blows the other direction back onto land. Also, Google the "dirty side of a hurricane" for more specific info as to which area gets the worst winds and rain.

Hurricane Milton is expected to encounter some wind shear as it moves closer to the Florida peninsula, particularly late Wednesday or early Thursday as it approaches landfall. This wind shear could cause some weakening before the storm fully makes landfall, but the exact timing and effect on the storm's intensity will depend on local atmospheric conditions, which can shift. Despite this, the storm is still projected to be a major system when it reaches Florida.
